name: Intune Toolkit
description: A PowerShell-based GUI solution for managing Microsoft Intune policies. Features Microsoft Graph authentication, multi-platform policy and app management, assignment operations, backup/restore in JSON format, CSV/Markdown export, and comprehensive activity logging.
author: Maxime Guillemin

securityCheck.aiSummary: Overall assessment: The Intune Toolkit script appears to be a legitimate administrative tool loading local modules and Graph-based context for Intune/Microsoft Graph interactions. There are no obfuscated commands, no remote-download execution, and no obvious credential harvesting. The primary security concerns are (1) logging/displaying PII (tenant and user) which could leak sensitive information if logs/UI are exposed, and (2) integrity risks from loading numerous local scripts without signing or integrity checks. Recommend redacting sensitive data in logs, enforcing script signing and integrity verification for all scripts in the Scripts directory, and validating that Graph tokens are handled securely with least-privilege scopes. Hardcoded secrets do not appear present, and no malicious patterns are detected beyond normal admin tooling behavior.
